questions.question: The world's longest railway platform is in:
questions.explanation: Gorakhpur railway station in Uttar Pradesh has the world's longest railway platform at 1,366.33 m. Kharagpur (1,072 m) was previously the record holder. Hubli also has a very long platform.

questions.question: India's busiest airport (by passenger traffic) is:
options: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi International Airport (Delhi) is India's busiest airport, handling over 70 million passengers annually. Mumbai's airport is the second busiest. Bengaluru is third.

questions.question: The Golden Quadrilateral highway project connects:
questions.explanation: The Golden Quadrilateral (GQ) connects Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata — India's four major cities — via 5,846 km of 4/6-lane National Highways. It is the largest highway project in India.

questions.question: The North-South and East-West Corridor highway project:
options: Connects Srinagar to Kanyakumari (N-S) and Porbandar to Silchar (E-W)
Links Northeast India to South India
questions.explanation: North-South Corridor: Srinagar (J&K) to Kanyakumari (Tamil Nadu) — 4,076 km. East-West Corridor: Porbandar (Gujarat) to Silchar (Assam) — 3,640 km. Together forming the 7,142 km backbone.

questions.question: Which Indian city has the largest natural harbour?
questions.explanation: Mumbai (Bombay) has one of the best natural harbours in the world. This is a key reason why it became India's commercial capital — it allowed large ships to dock safely. Vizag is India's deepest port.

questions.question: Which is India's longest road tunnel?
options: Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee Tunnel (Banihal-Qazigund, J&K)
questions.explanation: Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee Tunnel (Banihal-Qazigund Tunnel, 8.45 km rail tunnel) is part of the J&K railway. The Atal (Rohtang) Road Tunnel (9.02 km) is currently India's longest road tunnel.

questions.question: National Highway 48 (old NH-8) connects:
questions.explanation: NH-48 (formerly NH-8) is one of India's busiest highways, connecting Delhi to Mumbai (via Gurugram, Jaipur, Ajmer, Udaipur, Ahmedabad, Vadodara, Surat). Part of the Golden Quadrilateral.

questions.question: India's pharmaceutical hub (largest generic drug exporter) is:
questions.explanation: Hyderabad is India's top pharmaceutical hub ('Pharma City'), home to Dr. Reddy's, Cipla, Hetero, Aurobindo, Natco. India is the world's largest exporter of generic medicines.

questions.question: The Khyber Pass connects:
options: Pakistan (Peshawar) and Afghanistan (Kabul) — historic invasion route into India
questions.explanation: Khyber Pass (3.5 km wide at narrowest) connects Peshawar (Pakistan) with Kabul (Afghanistan). Historically the most important invasion route into the Indian subcontinent — used by Persians, Greeks, Mughals.

questions.question: Which state in India has no railway network?
options: Sikkim (no railway; Rangpo station being built to connect it)
questions.explanation: Sikkim was the only state with no railway network until recently. A rail line to Rangpo from West Bengal is being constructed. Sikkim is connected to the rest of India by NH-10 (Siliguri-Gangtok highway).

questions.question: India's largest port (by cargo handled) is:
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru Port (JNPT/Nhava Sheva) near Mumbai was India's largest container port for many years. However, Mundra Port (Adani Ports, Gujarat) has recently surpassed JNPT in total cargo.

questions.question: The Gulf of Kutch is famous for:
options: Tidal energy potential, Kandla Port, and flamingos
questions.explanation: Gulf of Kutch (Gujarat) has one of the highest tidal ranges in India (7-8 m), making it ideal for tidal energy. Kandla Port (Deendayal Port) is India's second-largest port by cargo. The gulf also has flamingos and corals.

questions.question: What is the primary mode of transport in the Himalayan region?
questions.explanation: Roads are the primary mode of transport in the Himalayan region due to its rugged terrain.
